name: Patriot Organizer

choices: Approach Thomas Greenleaf at the bar and question him about the operational plan's security.
Speak privately with Margaret Cole — she sees everything that happens in this tavern.
Leave immediately for the Counting House on King Street, where the operational documents are kept.
opening.narrative: Boston, December 16, 1773. The night air bites with a cold that feels like an omen — you have been in this city long enough to know the difference between a winter chill and the particular stillness that precedes catastrophe. For months, the Sons of Liberty have planned this night with painstaking care: 342 chests of East India Company tea, three ships, one harbor, and a message to Parliament that cannot be ignored. The cause of colonial liberty may hinge on what happens in the next few hours. But someone has talked. Samuel Adams pressed a note into your hand an hour ago — three words in an unfamiliar hand: 'One of yours.' You are standing in the Green Dragon Tavern, the fire low and the room thick with men who do not yet know the operation may already be compromised. Greenleaf is at the bar, his back to you, turning a coin over and over in his fingers. Margaret Cole is watching you from across the room with the expression of a woman who has already figured out more than she has said.

briefing: You are a trusted lieutenant of Samuel Adams with access to the Sons of Liberty's inner circle — and tonight, that access makes you a target. Identify the spy who has leaked tonight's operation before the boarding party reaches Griffin's Wharf. Fifteen minutes until Adams gives the signal. Name the wrong man and you fracture the movement. Name no one and the trap closes.
represents: Inner-circle lieutenants of Samuel Adams and the Sons of Liberty who organized and executed the Boston Tea Party on December 16, 1773
description: You are a trusted lieutenant of Samuel Adams, privy to the inner workings of the Sons of Liberty. You have access to the men who planned tonight's operation — but that also means the spy knows your face.
perspective: Write from the perspective of a senior Patriot operative — someone with authority, connections, and access to the inner circle of the Sons of Liberty. This player can press NPCs harder than others, invoke Samuel Adams's name, and access restricted areas like the Counting House without suspicion. However, their high profile means the spy is more likely to be on guard around them. Tone should be urgent and conspiratorial, with the weight of consequence in every exchange.
bridge_sentence: The chanting from the wharf has carried to the hill.
context_sentence: "You are Paul Revere, silversmith and dispatch rider for the Sons of Liberty, who on the night of December 16, 1773, coordinated the signal network between Old South Meeting House and Griffin's Wharf that launched the destruction of 342 chests of East India Company tea."

inventory: Samuel Adams's warning note (three words: 'One of yours')
Sons of Liberty membership token — a small copper disc

startingKnowledge: Samuel Adams received an anonymous warning note this afternoon suggesting there is a spy among the organizers.
The boarding party is scheduled to move from Old South to Griffin's Wharf the moment Adams gives the signal — you have perhaps fifteen minutes before that happens.
Thomas Greenleaf handles the Sons of Liberty's accounts and is one of only five men who knew the full operational plan.
